review_text:
It was terribly windy so it wasn't pleasant when the tent caved in. Your enjoyment is definitely dependent on the weather. I would suggest visiting in spring when it's daylight savings, otherwise the days are too short to enjoy the walks, beaches and wildlife.
Having the Visitor Centre and General Store on site are a huge advantage. A new building is being erected too. We look forward to seeing it when it's finished... but maybe from the warm confines of the cabins!
Relaxing and a dip in tidal river after hike

📍Awesome camping ground for the family.
📍Newly re-opened renovated general store with take away hot food.
📍Spectacular surroundings
📍Clean toilet/shower blocks
📍Great scenic walks and fishing
📍Plenty of birdlife and wombat visits
Nature in all its beauty

SO MANY CHILDREN SCREAMING. No relaxation whatsoever. Hikes and scenery are gorgeous but the camp itself is 80% school camps and very busy. I’d recommend any where else or just driving in for the day. We rarely pay for camping and this was definitely not worth the $37.
Excellent camp site. Drinking water taps located all around. Multiple bathroom blocks which are well looked after and large recycle bins available too.

Loved our little bush holiday here. The unpowered sites amongst the bushes are far superior to the powered gravel spots. So much wildlife will travel unperturbed through your site, just have to watch where you step as everywhere is their toilet 😆. Luckily we were due to leave on a windy day, wouldn't have been nice to stay with 100kmph winds! Beach close by is a bonus on hot days. The general store is handy to grab bits and pieces when you run out as it's a bit of a hassle going into the nearest town.
Great we go every year

We had terrible weather! A lot of dust flying around when it's windy (which stopped when the rain came) But the facilities are great: power outlets in the shower/toilet blocks,hot showers, toilets, laundry, and cafe. Close to the river and not too far to the beach. Both good for fishing.
Be sure to lock away your food in your car at night; possums and wombats will feast on it while you sleep otherwise (they'll even tear into your tent if the food is in there).

Wilsons Prom is such a special place with so many amazing walks and beaches.  The wildlife/bird life is fantastic.  Tidal River Campground, however, it's very run down.  Just little things could make a heap of difference.  Maybe a few extra soap dispensers in the ladies toilets? A hand dryer? They have quite a bit of space in the general store and it could potentially be very good, but the shelves are largely bare.
On saying that, we still had a lovely time.  Perfect place to stay with young kids. Right in the river and the beach. Spectacular!

National park looks lovely, but this campground needs some serious work. In windy weather like last night, the campground is completely unsuitable for tent camping. No shelter from the wind and sand/dust gets picked up and dumped everywhere, including in your tent. Considering that a lot of the sites are just sandpits, this is an issue.

Shame as it affected our sleep so badly we couldn’t really enjoy the national park today.
Perfect location, has showers, laundry room, cellular reception

Einfacher, aber sehr schöner Campingplatz.
Idyllisch gelegen am Fluss und am Meer.
Sehr schöne Wandermöglichkeiten.

In my well travelled opinion, Wilsons Promontory and Tidal River are one of the most beautiful places to visit and stay in Australia. The nature, beaches, walking tracks and wildlife are truly spectacular. I have been coming to Wilson's Promontory since I was a young teenager and recently took my son here for the first time. He absolutely loved it here and he is talking about coming back and exploring more.
There are many options for staying such as camping or staying in the cabins or the retreats that are on offer via the parks website.
Un lieu tres calme et agréable, les sanitaires sont propre, il manque juste une vrai cuisine il n'y a que des barbecues. On a eu la chance de croiser beaucoup de wombat !

Thankfully we were there while it was reasonably quiet. The sites are small and tight. I think it would be awful if it was fully booked.
They are doing renos on the toilet/shower blocks so we had to walk a long way to access them.
The showers had low pressure and were always dirty from just lots of people using them.
Location wise- the place is stunning. Great views. Right on the beach. The wildlife is amazing- LOVED the wombats!

Camping area a bit tired as was the castle block toilet block.
The area is over loved with too many visitors.  The national park is a beautiful place and requires a lot more TLC around the Camping area.
Great campground, for families and friends. Prices are discounted. Beaches are very safe but still keep an eye on your children. Low tide is perfect for non swimers. No fire's allowed nor pets.
Great place not to be missed
Best camping ground, many facilities and the best location in Victoria

An amazing camping site. Has everything you need and more. Spacious sites, amenity blocks (toilets with toilet paper, showers with hot water, dish washing station) well spaced out throughout the large campsite. Even has a grocery store, fish and chip shop, laundry and dryers, even an outdoor cinema! So much to do without needing to drive somewhere: beach, river activities, hike. 😊

Tidal River Campground is one of the most iconic camping destinations in Victoria, offering a unique mix of raw nature, coastal beauty, and easy access to some of the best spots in Wilsons Promontory National Park.

The campground is विशाल and well spread out, with hundreds of sites suitable for tents, caravans, and campervans. Surrounded by mountains, bushland, and the beautiful Tidal River itself, the setting feels peaceful and immersive. The river is shallow and calm in many areas, making it perfect for families and kids to safely enjoy the water.

One of the biggest highlights is the location. From your campsite, you’re just a short walk or drive to stunning beaches, scenic walking tracks, and wildlife spotting areas. Wombats, kangaroos, and birds are commonly seen around the campground, adding to the authentic outdoor experience.

Facilities are quite good for a national park campground. There are toilets, hot showers, laundry areas, dishwashing stations, and even BBQ facilities, along with a general store and café nearby for basic supplies and food  ￼. Recent upgrades and ongoing improvements to amenities are also being carried out to enhance the visitor experience  ￼.

However, it’s important to note that the campground can get very busy during peak seasons, and booking can sometimes be challenging. Some visitors have also mentioned that sites can be dusty with limited grass and that facilities, while functional, may feel slightly dated in parts  ￼.

Overall, Tidal River Campground offers an unforgettable camping experience with unbeatable natural surroundings. It’s perfect for those who love nature, beach access, and outdoor adventures. Whether you’re staying for a weekend or longer, it’s a must-visit spot for anyone exploring Victoria’s wilderness.
Nice camping area with a walking distance  beautiful beach. Highly recommended
Necessary building works spoils the vibe a little, but not too bad.

I had a wonderful stay at Tidal River in Wilsons Promontory National Park. It was such a special place to reconnect with nature — the landscape feels raw and grounding, and the park itself is very well looked after. You can really see the care that goes into maintaining it.

One memorable moment was when a wombat tried to sneak into our tent! It was incredibly windy, so we decided to quickly prepare a salad inside — apparently that was an open invitation. Definitely something to be mindful of, especially with food and wildlife around.

The cafe served delicious coffee, which was a treat for our mornings.

However, I was a little disappointed that compostable cups haven’t made it to the Prom yet… In a place so deeply connected to environmental values, it feels like a natural next step.

Overall, a beautiful and restorative experience — I’ll absolutely be back.
Busy but a great spot

To be honest, I've stayed in better free camps albeit without showers. The surroundings are beautiful but it's a carpark and not a cheap one at that. Be prepared for gusty winds and if you are unlucky enough sand blowing everywhere, especially if your site is a churned up sandpit. When I visit next time I will stay out of the park and make day trips.
There are some lovely hikes around the area and the campsite has good facilities( water and hot showers) However the campsite pitches are gravel and it gets extremely dusty when the wind picks up.

Facilities and Park Management: 1 Star. Something strange is happening here in the park. It looks like people are squeezed into one half of the park, which is overcrowded, and they are on top of each other, barely having enough space to park. The facilities are disgusting (dirty and stinky; we stayed for a few days, and no one cleaned them. You could catch something just by using them) On the other hand, the other side of the park is clean, and the campsites feel like VIP spots (maybe half are taken).

Nature and Views: 5 Stars.*****
There is no better place to camp than Tidal River Camp! Start packing and explore nature!
Cabins are starting to show signs of age but the location and atmosphere can’t be beaten
Amazing, enough said

Very crowded. Gravel/ dirt campsites so when gusty lots of dirt in the air. Very hard for tents. Each site on top of the next. Having said that, it’s very cheap and close to the beautiful surrounding walks, beaches, and broad walk. Showers were cleaned twice daily. So I would still support staying here as not really any other choice. The walks are breathtaking and the wildlife very present. I’d rate it a 5 for walks and area and a 3 for motor homes; a 2 if ur camping in tents.
Gorgeous place to camp at reasonable rates. Small sites and quite busy but you can see why. Camp here at least once in your life ❤️

Book in advance! As very often fully booked. Pick your spot wisely. I started off on 3rd avenue which was a large gravel car park with minimal privacy or shade and dusty in the wind. I finished my stay on 25th avenue which was closer to the beach and nicer with some trees and more privacy. However both spots did serve me and it wouldn't put me off coming again. Warm showers and drinking water available. Signal all over the site for phones. Amazing stars at night. Really beautiful beach for swimming or kayaking ect. Wonderful sunsets. The beach was the absolute highlight as well as the hikes around. A truly gorgeous area to explore. I would normally choose a quieter campsite but there are limited options in this area and I would certainly come back. Very clean and well maintained. I understand why it gets so busy! Wilson's promontory is spectacular with lots of ocean/ mountain views. I felt safe here and it was a good recharge before heading back to more secluded spots. Small shop and coffee shop on site. Boat tours also available.
We had a lovely campsite in a perfect location. Good facilities nearby and a very good size for our group.
One of the best national parks in Victoria.

Zelf heb ik een hele fijne ervaring hier gehad en moet ik toegeven dat ik het jammer vind dat we door moesten met onze reis.

Qua voorzieningen is het prima, toiletten werken allemaal goed (stonken wel bij de mannen), de douches zijn goed en warm, geen problemen gehad met water, het kleine winkeltje heeft alles wat je echt nodig hebt. Het is schoon genoeg, ik vind het allemaal goed.

De locatie zelf is natuurlijk prachtig, ik hoef daar denk ik niet te lang over door gaan. Dicht bij zee, bergen, en alle dieren die je je maar kan bedenken!

Ik had een hangmat bij mijn tent opgehangen. Een medewerker van de camping kwam naar me toe en vertelde dat dit helaas niet mag. Dit deed hij zó vriendelijk. Ik merkte aan hem dat hij het eigenlijk niet wilde zeggen, maar dat het wel moet. Natuurlijk direct geluisterd en de hangmat verwijderd.

De balie medewerker was ook heel vriendelijk naar ons. De meiden bij het winkeltje vond ik echter minder vriendelijk; het kwam over alsof ze niet zoveel zin hadden om te werken.  Begrijpelijk op die leeftijd, maar niet uitnodigend om terug te komen.

Al met al vond ik het wel 5 sterren waard en kom ik heel graag terug.

Lack of parking, lack of rubbish bins and very cramped. No shade anywhere and dust blows around continuously. The toilets are a filthy disgrace but at least they are flush toilets - for the few that bother to use that function.
Everyone visiting the area just wanders through the campsite so nothing can be left unattended and you need to close/lock everything so not ideal with the hot weather. Dust blows everywhere continuously so overall it's a toss up between whether a roadside gravel pit or here would rate higher - definitely not worth the money to stay here.

Los espacios comunes muy limpios. Nos tocó una parcela sin pasto lo cual no estuvo tan cómodo.
La playa y el río son hermosos. Muchos wombats por la noche, ideal para conocer más sobre la fauna Aussie.
Great place for camping

We went fully self sufficient but decided to dine in the restaurant. It was terrible. Nothing has changed for years. There’s almost nothing green or not deep fried on the menu. And, the dining room has comically restrictive hours. They clearly have no real interest in serving the campers.
Go swim or fly a kite

Good toilets and a general store (rarity among campgrounds), but this is not it as a nature escape. I always check in here for the southern hikes, but from what I can see, you are camping on a very large, flat field with no view other than the RVs next to you.

Vibe is akin to a tent city outside of an outdoor rave party. In summer especially it is as populated as Melbourne central. Might be enjoyable to some and certainly the nearby beach and walks are gorgeous, but I wouldn't recommend it just for camping.
Beautiful as always :)
Run like the Victorian Government. Overflowing bins scruffy facilities and construction overruns but the area is fantastic
I have been coming over here since past 15 years. Amazing camp ground and all facilities for camping are available. Need a booking way in advance but worth it.

Booked from 19-26 December. Had to leave on the 23rd as the weather was going to get worse.  There were wind gusts up to 70kmh. There should be a warning system for campers. Some people suffered damage to their tents and gazebos. Hope no one got hurt. Not happy having booked 7 days. They should offer refunds if it is unsafe to stay.

Nice place but always packed with people.
No fires are allowed and it is cold almost every night here. Even in summer

Large campground with plenty of facilities, located next to the river and the beach.
Things I found useful to know:
•	There are hot showers.
•	Laundry facilities are available.
•	Powered sites aren’t anything special; unpowered sites are much nicer.
•	Powered sites don’t have fresh-water supply or grey-water disposal. You can use the central facilities for that.
•	There is a lot of construction on and around the campground, and some walking tracks are closed (November 2025). Check the campground’s website for up-to-date information.
•	There’s a small convenience store.
•	You can get good coffee there!
•	The shuttle bus to Telegraph Saddle — the starting point for the Mount Oberon summit hike — only runs on weekends at this time of year.
•	The information boards are outdated (in November they still showed information from April), but the staff at the visitor centre are very helpful.
- (public) Dump point is at Overnight Hikers Car Park, next to camping
•	Most people do spot wombats around dusk and dawn on the campground (not in a fixed spot), but unfortunately I didn’t see any — but that’s wildlife :-)
Free camping with water access, a small store, wildlife running around and a beautiful beach. Insane that something like this exists

We stayed for 4 days. First two were amazing, swam hiked and whatnot. Lovely weather and we got an amazing campground in the shade. I did however see that other campgrounds resembled parking lots, having nothing but gravel. Choose carefully guys. Second two days were rough with winds, most people left. The experience is very weather dependent, can't really do much when there is dust blown around in extreme winds. Make sure to check the weather for the region when you go. Lovely views though, worth it. Bathrooms were relatively clean and there is even laundry rooms, so great amenities.
Wombats!!!

Great camp grounds and facilities.
However… can be horrendously exposed should any wind or rain come through!
Double check your pitch booking on the Vic app if it might rain etc

Stunning location. Amazing place for walks and beach/river. The camp spot has 300+ spots and for what you pay it’s underwhelming and you just wish parks vic could do better. Very weather dependant, wouldn’t go on high wind or wet days. Good Telstra reception. If conditions are good this is a magical place to take kids. The parks vic camping system is awful so you just book and cross your fingers with the weather because if you cancel with a week to go you get no refund so why cancel, hence all the ghost spots

Tidal River Campground ist immer wieder ein Besuch wert! Die Natur ist unbeschreiblich schön, es gibt viele sehr gut gepflegte Wanderwege rund um den Campingplatz. Am Tidal River Inlett können die Kinder sicher und gut spielen. Man kann auch sehr erfolgreich fischen auf den extra angelegten Holzplatformen am Tidal River.

Einzig die Sanitäreinrichtungen sind etwas in die Jahre gekommen. Alles in allem wird der Campingplatz aber gut unterhalten.
Great spot to go camping and see wildlife
Great place for camping and kayaking
Excellent country. Good camp site that gets very busy!
Great location with clean amenities.  Parking and information centre here for walking maps which is great for tourists or day trips
It looks like it needs some maintenance. Old looking and just a few BBQs when there are so many people. The good thing about it are the animals wandering around at dusk.

I adore the Prom BUT the current system where bookings are taken only online and indicate 'full' is not a true reflection of the actual attendance. It seems many are taking the online system as a means of securing a site by paying but then not committing to attend. Whilst this maintains a revenue stream (which no doubt is needed) it also means that the less appealing sites (especially atm whilst construction is on) are being utilised to the max in less than ideal locations. Construction noise is high, a car's horn is blasting on and off all the time. The flow on effects are very clear - the cafe is low in supplies...how challenging is it when bookings reflect 'full' and yet uptake is much less. Please re think this booking system. I vote for paying a significant amount more to book as a deposit with a refund on attendance.

Lots of people so can be loud but expected. Amenities are very good even with temporary bathrooms in some sections atm, can have hot shower, normal toilets with tp & soap, beach showers to rinse off, public BBQs, Also water available for drinking etc.

Nice to stay closer to the beach ramps if surfing so don't have to carry boards too far.

Lots of wildlife, wombats, wallabies & birds easy to see

Für den Preis gerade noch okay, was Ausstattung und Service betrifft.  Die Lage ist sehr gut als Ausgangspunkt für Wanderungen im Wilsons Pr. NP.
Die Plätze für Camper sind sehr unterschiedlich: vom schnöden Parkplatz als Stellfläche bis zu schönen,  grünen Nischen gibt es alles. Beim online Buchen sieht man aber nicht was man bekommt.
Plätze mit Strom und Wasser gibt es viel zu wenig. Es gibt keine Dumpstation.
Als wir da waren, gab es einige Baustellen, leider war auch Brücke über den Tidal gesperrt.

A great place to stay for easy access to many wonderful walks. The shower block facilities were well maintained and a cafe is perfect for a morning coffee. There is also an information centre next door and they have a wealth of knowledge about the area. We stayed in an AVan and the spot Ewa’s very spacious with easy access to power and water.
Great base for exploring at Wilson's Promontory. Clean amenities, small cafe, small grocers, lots of sites although they are a tad short for caravans and tight to get into.

Tidal river camp ground is a reliable and affordable way to experience all that the Prom has to offer.
The bird life and Australian mammals are abundant and the views are spectacular. The beach is easily accessible.
The greenery surrounding tidal river is fantastic, although it does not compare to the civic green.
Would recommend for a weekend getaway or a longer stay.

Beautiful area of Australia with very well set up camp sites. Lots of wildlife including wombats and birds or all kinds. Have to book a long way out and it’s competitive but there are lots of walks nearby and rivers and beaches.

Awful. Over 400 tiny sites, crammed close together, complete with general store, construction  sites, and other commercial operations. The rangers in the "information centre" did everything they could not meet our eye when we ventured in. No greeting, no offerings, it's basically a call centre. (And while I appreciate a better, fit for purpose centre is being built, the staff will need to overall their service attitude and culture to make anyone feel welcome.)  The footbridge across the river to the walks from the camp is closed, so the pressure on alternative routes and carparks even midweek was huge. The views, the bush and the beaches are lovely but it's hard to appreciate it amongst the throngs.
Could be improved by expanding the powered sites and having more shrubs between sites.
Great campground with water and electricity. Perfect for motorhomes and caravans.
One of the best camping places. Covered BBQ, lots of wild life, surfing, hiking, good prices for camping, drinkable water on all taps.

Great for tent and car camping, not bad for camper-trailers, but spaces can be really tight for full-size caravans. Abundant toilet and shower facilities. Very close to flat river bed and beach. Relatively safe for kids.

Victorian's bread and butter camp. Very solid emvironment for easy access around Wilson's Prom. Flushing toilets are reasonably clean and you have hot showers, laundry and a shop with every supply you can imagine. 10/10.

Excellent place for camping, safe  beach for kids, walk trail, stay with facilities like toilet blocks, general store with a cafe, a visitor information centre and bus for Mount Oberon. Currently the facilities are been upgraded, hence few walk trails are closed.
Very nice camping site, with all facilities you need and super nice people.
Excellent experience
Beautiful area hamstrung by the lack of government financial support

Very big, but great campground. Many nice walks in the area. Tip: take a stroll after dark around the campsite: you will see many wombats and probably some possums too. Really feels like you're camping in the middle of nature, nice!
If you go once, you’ll be back
amazing place to camp

Our favorite group camping place. Don't leave any food or traces of food in tents unless you want wombats ripping through your tent and raiding.

Love walking along the river, beach coast, little and long hiking trails here and there.

Good place for beginner campers since there it has some of the best state run facilities eg flush toilet, BBQ, laundry, kitchen washing basin, warm showers.

Roadtrip and rest-stop at Tidal River on a cold Sunday 29/6.  Camp ground still busy and infrastructure being developed.  Egg and Bacon Rolls, Hash Browns and two hot coffees from the General Store were just the ticket for breckky/ morning tea.  Too wet for good photos.  Just one at Squaky Beach.  Saw Emus but no wombats.
Went here over the long weekend. Amazing beaches and scenery. Keep rubbish up high, otherwise the abundant wombats will get into it.
Beautiful spot in the park — wombat heaven at night!

Süße Wombats über den ganzen Platz verteilt.
Süße Vögel und hier und da sogar Wallaby’s 😍😍
A must see,  just beautiful,  so much to see.
Great campground, large area. Cafe on sight, information centre. Cinema, river behind camping area. Lots of wombats and kangaroos and birdlife.
Excellent spot for family camping , lots of facilities clean facilities

Beautiful place as always. But you can see how poorly funded Parks Victoria is. It's very disappointing that one of Victoria's tourism jewels is in pretty poor shape, as far as maintenance and facilities.

Tidal River Playground is such a great place for kids and families. There’s lots of space to run around, and the playground itself is safe, fun, and surrounded by nature. We even saw wombats and wallabies wandering nearby—such a treat for the little ones!

The whole area is very well maintained. The public toilets and other facilities were spotless, even during the busy weekend. There's also a market and a general store close by, which came in super handy for snacks, ice cream, and a few camping essentials.

Great place to relax, explore, and enjoy nature without sacrificing comfort. Highly recommended if you're visiting Wilsons Prom!

It's a shame people book a site and can't be bothered turning up or cancelling and not contacting npw so many free sites.
Anyway our site is fantastic

The Tidal river campground had stunning and well cared for facilities with great views around Norman beach and Tidal River to appreciate. Our group saw an abundance of birdlife and the natural surroundings created a peaceful and serene enviroment in which to eat lunch in. There are a good number of tables and picnic facilities however not too much built factors that it takes away the enjoyment of being in a national park. Would highly recommend if you are in the area around Wilson's prom and looking for a few days of camping or even just a nice lunch spot.
Wunderschöner Campingplatz, off season kann man dort für 0€ Campen, man muss nur online reservieren
Clean facilities, the park was pretty busy but nevertheless there was enough space and night time was quiet. Bautiful beach and riverside closeby.
Marvellous place to visit, excellent walks , Mt Oberon has spectacular views .
Had a good camping experience at Easter long weekend.

Unfortunately we arrived on the long weekend (Easter), it was too crowded, with not enough room on road due to cars being parked both sides
When we found a park, we had a look at the beach, absolutely stunning, we tried to look at squeaky beach, but traffic’s worse.
Will have to go off season

This place is amazing and the staff are more than helpful.  We did the boat ride out to the seals and scull rock, a great time had by all. The thing that I find absolutely disgusting is that people have booked and just not turned up and have been too selfish to cancel their bookings. It's Anzac day and the park says its full on the website when actually it is only about half full. Their are powered and non powered sites empty and have been for 3 days now. We had to move our van from a powered site 2 days ago as it was "BOOKED" still no one on that site. Parks Victoria have done a great thing by offering these great spots free, only to be abused by self centred people. Shame on you all that booked and never turned up and couldn't be bothered to cancel, preventing other people that dearly wanted to come but couldn't.

Magnifique endroit subventionné par le gouvernement et donc gratuit. Eau chaude, beaucoup d’emplacement, le lieu est chaleureux, équipé de machine à laver et possède même une supérette à proximité. Tellement pratique 😍

This beach is beautifully clean and peaceful—an absolute gem for early morning strolls or serene evening escapes. It's also one of the best spots to camp under the stars and soak in the natural beauty all around

Great place, unfortunately the weather was not great, very windy and rain. Due to people not cancelling their, currently free, camp site. I couldn't change to a more protected site, even though no one came for it.(About 2/3 of the campground was empty), but I still enjoyed a walk on the beach. Also, the footbridge is closed atm, which is a shame, but I guess safety first.
Sehr schön, da gerade gratis. Etwas unübersichtlich und die Toiletten könnten sauberer sein.
The best spot to relax and escape

Very nice campground
Clean toilets and showers
Great spot but why are there no BBQs on bbq road 😭😭😭😭😭

We used to love this place with all the facilities of hot water, toilet, all clean around, BBQ all clean But this was our third visit to Tidel River Campground, but unfortunately, it turned into a distressing experience. My husband and I had booked a stay from March 31st to April 4th, 2025, at site 166 on 5th Avenue. On the night of March 31st, while we were asleep, an individual attempted to open our tent around midnight and tried to steal something. Thankfully, I heard a noise, woke up suddenly, and yelled, "Who are you?" I was shaken for the rest of the night, and my husband decided it was best for us to cut our trip short and return home the following day.

During our stay, we noticed there was no ranger or security presence on-site at night, which contributed to our sense of insecurity.

Please stay vigilant and stay safe, everyone.
Specially who is planning to stay in the tent 🙏🏻
Great place

Have been coming here since a child. Summer holidays in the safety of patrolled beaches, and the river, hiking. Cooler climate than the city in the peak of summer heatwaves. And multi day hikes through unique ecosystems and stunning vistas.

Title: A Memorable Stay at Tidal River Campground, Wilson's Promontory – A Detailed Review

I recently had the pleasure of staying at Tidal River Campground, located in the heart of Wilson’s Promontory National Park, and I must say, it was an experience that truly exceeded my expectations. Tidal River is a hub for visitors exploring the park, offering an incredible base to immerse yourself in nature, enjoy the surrounding landscapes, and relax in a peaceful, family-friendly environment. Over the course of my stay, I was able to explore various aspects of the campground and the surrounding area, and I thought I would share my detailed review to help future campers make the most of their time here.

Location and Scenic Beauty: A Nature Lover's Dream

One of the most striking aspects of Tidal River Campground is its location. Nestled on the edge of the river with a view of the picturesque Wilsons Promontory coastline, the campground is beautifully positioned within the national park. The surrounding environment is nothing short of stunning, with towering mountains, thick forests, and the serene Tidal River offering a tranquil atmosphere. You feel as though you’re completely immersed in nature the moment you arrive, with the constant sound of waves crashing in the distance, birds chirping, and the gentle rustle of trees in the breeze.

It’s also worth noting that the campground is just a short distance from some of the park’s most famous landmarks, such as Squeaky Beach, the Mount Oberon Summit, and Norman Beach. If you’re an avid hiker or nature enthusiast, the location makes it incredibly easy to access the park’s many trails, making it the perfect spot to base yourself for a few days of exploration.

The Campground Layout and Amenities: Well-Equipped and Comfortable

Tidal River Campground is one of the largest campgrounds in the park, offering a variety of accommodation options to suit different preferences. Whether you're into traditional tent camping, staying in a caravan, or renting a cabin, there is something for everyone here. There are approximately 480 campsites spread out in a relatively spacious layout, ensuring that even during peak season, it doesn’t feel overcrowded. The sites are carefully organized, allowing for privacy between campers and making it easy to relax in your space.

The sites themselves are generally level, with good access to basic facilities like picnic tables, fire pits (depending on the season and fire restrictions), and BBQ areas. For those with tents, many sites are shaded by tall trees, providing some welcome relief from the sun during the warmer months. The proximity to the river means that some campsites offer excellent views, making it a peaceful spot to sit back and enjoy nature.

The park itself is meticulously maintained, and the staff at Tidal River take great care in ensuring the area remains clean and welcoming. There are clearly marked paths and easy access to shared facilities like toilets, showers, and drinking water stations, which are all kept in excellent condition. A key highlight is the cleanliness of the campground, as it’s evident that a lot of effort goes into maintaining the area, especially given the large number of visitors.

Facilities and Services: Convenience in the Wilderness

Tidal River Campground offers a wide range of facilities that add convenience to your stay, making it an excellent spot for both experienced campers and families looking for a comfortable base to explore the park. Some of the key amenities include:

1. General Store: The Tidal River General Store is a great resource for last-minute supplies. They stock a range of groceries, including fresh produce, snacks, canned goods, and camping essentials like firewood, fuel, and gas. If you’ve forgotten something, it’s a reliable place to grab what you need without having to leave the park.
Place is well maintained. However the location is too windy. Come prepared. Lots activites around this place.

Very nice campground

Ground is a little hard if you are in a swag or tent

Ghost campers are a pox that the regulator needs to fix. Drove into the park with a sign saying all camp spots full, get there and the place is half empty. Here's a suggestion, at 6.00am each morning Rangers do a lap of the park, any site empty that is meant to be occupied has its booking cancelled and the email address blocked for future bookings.
Beautiful place to camp with the family. Best beach/river combo in Victoria.

Bon camping, il faut aimer la proximité avec les autres mais franchement c’est pas si pire que d’autres, au moins on est super proches de toutes les randonnées et des plages ! Eau potable, électricité, eau chaude, tout ça gratuit (en ce moment), incroyable !!

Hard to find available free spots for camping, so I’d recommend booking in advance. Great place to stay if you really wanna be within nature, but still have some basic facilities. Showers and toilets were always clean. There’s lots of wildlife and the river and beach are beautiful.

Perfect camp BUT
The only problem is the campsites being free so the camping is always fully booked with ghost campers...
Would prefer to pay 20 bucks so people would not abusively book.

Lovely campsite, with nice facilities (toilets, hot shower and a general store) also plenty of hikes you can do around the area!
Campground is free, just make sure to book early.

The area is beautiful, beaches and mountains with lush forests!
Ein toller Camping Platz in der Natur
Wonderful place but if you go don’t go for day trip go for 2/3 days

Stunning location and decent camp set up. Facilities and signage need some work - clearer signage would be a big help when you first arrive. Some aspects of facilities are dated or broken and not fixed but it's free for tents and overall really nice. Amazing location and all the water is potable. Sure everyone else has mentioned this but the wildlife are 'assertive' so lock up everything that might be remotely edible at night.
Beautiful place,heaps of trail walks and the night stargazing was amazing.
The prom and this campsite never disappoints. Surrounded by tidal river and Norman beach. Well maintained campground with excellent facilities. Very friendly rangers.
Lovely spot to camp. Reservation required. Amenities a bit dated.
Beautiful campground, most sites grassed and surrounded by vegetation. Ample amenities blocks.
Tidal River has it all nice shallow spots for young & old. Sun & surf a quick 5min walk from camp Park. Very busy on long weekend but still worth the effort get there early.
The place to enjoy nature to its fullest.

First of all Wilson’s Prom is a beautiful place to visit and you won’t be disappointed coming down here. Immediate proximity to Tidal River and Norman’s Beach is so great. The facilities are fine but there was always a wait for the toilets and showers. However the campsites themselves are not recommended for caravans and if I had a tent as well I would think twice about coming here. We had a powered site and I felt more as if I was in a carpark rather than a beautiful wilderness. We came down over Australia Day weekend and it was rammed with cars everywhere and not safe for kids to ride on their bikes. The sites are on top of each other and little place to park etc. It was super dusty and windy and we left a night early as did many with twisted and dirty tents. If you must come down here then come down when it’s not busy and would stay at beach sites which are less busy and dusty but also less amenities.

It’s been more than four months that we’ve been trying to book a spot, but it always says unavailable. I’m not sure if it’s an issue with the system or if it’s actually fully booked. I even tried booking for next year, but it’s already booked until 2026🤨❓
Beautiful part of Victoria

Бесплатные зоны.
Санитарные узлы чисто.
Локация отличное
Beautiful lookout.
My best holiday destination
It's a beautiful family camping and swimming place.

A great campsite and an amazing getaway from busy Melbourne. The campground itself has great shower blocks, with a couple of small ones and a few large ones.

The surrounding areas include Norman Beach, a great beach with plenty of space and fairly chill water. Tidal River, which is a calm river with a shallow point for swimming in and a deeper part perfect for paddle boarding. There are also many very scenic hikes/walks around the area.

Squeaky Beach, which happened to be the best-rated beach in Victoria last year, is also a three-minute drive from the campground and yes, it does squeak.

I come camping here every year and planning on going next week. Happy camping!

Nature is fantastic down there. Lots of vombats in the campground at night, wonderful hikes etc.

The campground itself is relatively poorly run. I'd recommend hiring a campground leader/manager. Toilets are cleaned too irregularly in high season. We were there mid December, and the information posters found around the campground was "whats on this week" for the week of October 13th, so ~8 weeks too old, and in the middle of high season.

The staff that is working there is doing a good job, but I think there's a lack of leadership.

Since its a defacto monopoly on camping in the area, then this is still your best place to go, and I'd recommend it.

I'd avoid Avenue 1-5 (most spots are super cramped together and dusty) and try to get something on 6+ Avenue...
Just had the best time! Great spot for activities and relaxation!

Beautiful family friendly camp site. Great facilities such as washing & drying for clothes( $4 per a session) hot water , drinking water , café near by , charging station. Please make sure you DO NOT keep food inside tents unless airtight container containers if not 50 kg wombat will come inside without an invitation 🥰. Non - powered for camp sites so solar or battery power needs to be organised by yourself . ( toilets area do have power & charging station is free )
Too many visitors! Sites on mud or hard soil... I ask why is this camp so frequented... Only few powered sites but some huts. From my point of view not really to recommend!
Good 👍

Very nice camping site and can almost consider this for glamping (only site with cinemas!). Always our option to bring visitors/family member from overseas as introduction to camping. We suggest staying at least 2 nights for 3-hour drive's worth.
Great campground, lots of places and good amenities

We stayed in a hut before heading off onto the light house. It was a small but comfortable hut with enough to do basic cooking but BYO bedding. The campground had a variety of accomodation with a general store and cafe that provided a reasonable selection of goods and hot food. The staff were all very friendly and will to answer any questions
Love this place, it’s clean and a great escape from the busy city lifestyle!
Lovely family atmosphere. Beautiful beaches and hiking trails.
Have proper amenities, plenty of sites but need to book early if u wanna spend the long weekend holiday, our site was 34, pretty good
Stayed here in our caravan in August. So, not overly crowded. We had a  powered site for 2 nights. Lots of native wildlife & amazing walks. The Toilets block was a bit dated. Cafe. T.V  Internet.

It is very nice place to stay everything is perfect and nice. Just One thing to keep in mind. If you are planning to use a tent ⛺️ please be careful as Wombat can come and able to scratch your tent and try to come inside for food. We had a bad experience of wombat and then we slept in the car whole night. So please keep this thing in your mind. Otherwise all the facilities and everything is perfect.

A unique place that can satisfy any group with different likings and favourite activities.
You can hike to the top of the mountain, swim or walk along the river. Have a bbq in the camp ground. Or take a dip in the ocean. A good get-away place that is not too far from the city.
Schöner Campground zu fairem Preis

Went for a day trip to Wilson Prom and ended up for a stop at Tidal River Campground area. Public toilet was clean and the area was nice and quiet. We were particularly thrilled by the colorful and friendly parrots in the area (we took some selfies with them) as well as mr. wombat who kept on munching those grasses like there is no tomorrow. Such a lovely place. :)

We used to love camping at this location year after year. However, last year, our experience was marred when the new booking system crashed just as we tried to reserve our usual spot. When it finally came back online, everything showed as unavailable.

This year, we were informed of a new booking system touted as "the fairest system." My wife and I both tried to book starting at 10 AM, 8th August. Despite initially showing availability, every attempt to book our usual spot or any nearby sites resulted in messages saying they were no longer available. We tried everything listed as "available" to no avail.

Instead of joy, relaxation, and happy family memories, the new system achieved the opposite effect. We find this so-called fair system to be unfair, unfriendly to customers, and extremely frustrating. The old ballot system we found to be fair enough, and also stress free.

Sadly, it seems our family holidays at Tidal River may be a thing of the past - And our camping friends that we met every year will also be.

Super camping pour visiter le parc national, beaucoup (beaucoup) d'emplacement, choisissez bien là où vous préférez être dans le camping via la réservation en ligne. Les emplacements sont très bien, l'accès direct à la plage est top, les toilettes et les douches sont propres également. Nous avons même eu l'occasion de croiser 2 wombats autour de notre van durant notre séjour.

Went car camping for one night here. An underrated aspect about this campsite is how there was little/no light pollution here, meaning you’ll be blessed with the most gorgeous display of stars not too far from the CBD!

Photos are just shot from an iPhone - no professional equipment required
Lovely place, ruined by too many campers and muddy wet sites and road with pot holes
One of the best campgrounds! The toilet and facilities are well maintained and lots of places to visit around and
Great place for lovers of the outdoors...

Gorgeous NP with lots to discover so definitely recommend spending the night here. I think they closed the area close to the beach in low season because couldn't make a reservation for any of those spots and didn't see any cars parked over there. We had campsite 189, bit small but close to facilities. We had a resident possum it seemed, nice fella. Also many wombats everywhere. Toilets clean, showers hot and good pressure. Overall great place! Oh and take a walk to Norman beach at night for some stargazing!
Great spot. Fair price and lots to do from here
one of the best campsites ever ❤️ will def come back ❤️ Thanks for having us
Lovely camp site, great for families,  access to beach and plenty of walks

Stayed for three nights on an unpowered site. Our particular site had bushes at the front so we were lucky to only have people camping on one side of us as we would have been stuck in if both sides were occupied!

Shower blocks are dated but functional and hot water was nice. A few extra hooks in the showers would help. Shower blocks are scattered throughout so you don't have to walk far to a toilet or shower. They also have laundry facilities and sinks for washing dishes.

The general store has most grocery items if you forget something and the Cafe sells nice hot chips and coffee which is great after a walk in the cool weather.

There is also an outdoor cinema at certain times if you like that.

Camp ground is clean and tidy with plenty of rubbish and recycling bins so no excuse for leaving a mess behind.

I've been here lots of times since I was a kid and really love the river and beaches. It's a beautiful place and great place to camp.

would be good if there r more power sites.
great for kids!

Fantastic location and the Parks Victoria staff are wonderful,  especially in the General Store. Toilets and showers are cleaned daily,  twice daily on busy days.
There are only a few powered sites, so book early and BYO grey water tank or bucket.
Don't expect it to be like a Big4 or other commercial park, there is no playground or swimming pool, but there is a laundry and BBQs.
You don't need a pool as one of most beautiful beaches in Australia is only a few minutes walk from the campground.
No dogs allowed as it's a National Park.

The campground is perfectly located right at the foot of Mount Oberon in the middle of nature with access to the beach. It advertises lots of wombats, which we can definitely confirm. We had a great stay for two nights and can definitely recommend the campground.

I’ve stayed here for 2 nights (9th-11th Apr 24). Picnic area,Camping area , BBQ area and toilet were really nice and look cleaned **but it’s not safety 100%**. I’ve been there with my best friend( 2 ladies me and my friend)

While we were sleeping in the night around3 am. Somebody tried to steal something from our tent. He put his hand get through window side of my tent. But it was lucky. I’d heard some noise and woke up suddenly and scream “who are you?” Then I woke up my friend. After that the thief he was outside still. and waited for us to fall asleep again ( I heard his steps walk around our tent). So I decided to call the police and they said they would contact someone at Tidal river service. Then i’d been waiting for 25mins. The Tidal river staffs came and told us to calm down ,gave us a contact number then they checked around because sometime it’s just our imagine. The staffs advised us to leave in the morning because the road was not safe

I’d checked and I found out that the thief cut  window side’s tent and we found his footprints around our tent

Can you imagine that? what would happen with us if i’m not careful?  I hope it would be more safety in the future and if put the emergency contact number around the place(contact number for tidal river service). It should be someone work on night shift there to response in urgent situation quickly.

Love the Prom.
Swimming, walking, paddling.
Great place to camp, expect to be packed in like sardines over busyweekends.

Great Camping site with mountains river and sea. Hiking , swimming or surfing options and great shallow river where kids can play or deeper water for paddle boards. Great place for family or friends gathering too. Lots to do around the campground.
An absolutely stunning spot!

I loved this campground! I mean, it can be pretty crowdy, but still it’s a lovely place. The surroundings are just stunning. The beach is very beautiful with a forest on one side and large red stones on the other. Also the wombats walking around at night on the campground and the birds visiting your outside table make your camping adventure unforgettable!
The campground needs some work very very dusty. More signals on walks
Only parked there to access the beach and get something for lunch. Very popular spot in a beautiful part of Australia.  Lots of amenities
Great camping ground. We saw wombats walking around on the campsite. The facilities of the campground felt like the facilities of a holiday park - fantastic!
Super 👍 Toller Strand und Sanitär ist 👍

4 stars only because the nature, wildlife and beach were so beautiful.

Things to note:
- Very dirty and old amenities
- No camping kitchen
- Very, very many children with ignorant parents: lots of shouting late into the night, chasing wildlife :((
Stunning national park with mountains to hike, bush land to explore and camping where the river meets the sea. Magical location!
They need more powered sites
This is much favorite place for camping. Tidal river is absolutely beautiful with the red rocks and the ocean at the end. Just love it 😍 and so many wombat around the tent 🏕️😁
The campground is being overpopulated, there is no time for rejuvenating the grass and dust is a huge problem. Reduce numbers so areas can be rested
I was a daytripper for photography with a Nikon D850 camera

Wilsons promatory is one of my favourite places anywhere, Tidal River camp round is a big part, with over 250 campsites, which get booked out as they did last weekend. Lots of wildlife including many wombats birds and possums. The wombats are great but entered our mesh fly tent though the mesh in the night, no food in there, they will raid your camp looking for food. General store and take away fills some gaps. Many excellent walks originate from Tidal river.
Best Beach I have ever had enjoyed

Great camp ground,  some powered sites, but book early.!!
The walks and scenery are beautiful. Well done Parks.!
Plenty of amenitie blocks. Nice Cafe, but it can get very busy. Plenty to do and see...

It's always a great decision to camp here.  People respect the noise levels, except at New Years Eve, obviously. I've never seen rubbish floating around, and I've been going since I was a kid. But less over the last 10 yrs. I make a day trip whenever I'm visiting family in Vic, and the shop has usually changed again..lol. The tshirts could be more vibrant.
Wahnsinns Park , waren 2 Nächte hier, super sauber , hatten Glück noch nen Platz zu bekommen
Our favourite camping spot. The tidal beach is breathtaking and very safe to swim.
Amazing

Tidal River Campground is just awesome for camping! It's super chill for families, and there's always something fun going on. The natural surroundings make it a rad spot for hiking, and they've got cool group activities too. The whole vibe is laid-back, and the facilities are on point. If you're into a mix of nature and good times, Tidal River is the place to be! 🏞️🏕️
Very nice place to visit.
Hard to find parking but Tidal river is good place to be
Best camping site
We will come back again.
澳洲大陆的最南端，海上的乌鲁鲁石头，超震撼。

Amazing campsite and well maintained. It had hot water facility, clean and maintained toilets, friendly parks Victoria members ready to assist. Big campsite. Seperate place for washing utensils. Power points available near laundry area. Washing machine and dryer facilities available. Next to the beautiful beach and tidal river. You can enjoy multiple sports on the beach and at the river. Tidal river is very nice for swim. Few bush walks around the campsites are amazing. Watch out for wombats, though they are friendly but can attract if they smell food in tent. Convenient store has all basic you need, it also has a cafe and few food options available. Toilets are cleaned everyday. Campsites also has open cinema if anyone wish to watch.

Considering the sheer volume of visitors, actually really well-managed and spaced out.

Obviously, a great location and time for anyone. But for anyone worrying that this campsite might be low quality or a bit unkempt, it isn’t! Anything but!

Great water pressure in the showers, wash up facilities, spacious and private sites for motor homes. Good bbq facilities and access to many things!

Amazing campground at the National Park. Lots of sites and lots of people but beautiful location with good hikes and tidal river and ocean close. One of the biggest campgrounds you will ever go to probably. Great spot. We stayed 2 nights. There is fresh water available and a visitor center and small store for essentials only. Good size sites.

I intended to do an overnight hike on The Prom Southern Circuit.
(Solo bushwalker).

Ideally I wanted to get in late the night before my walk begins, early the next day.

Within Tidal River there is a  "Overnight Hikers' Campsite. I have stayed within it before, years ago. The campsites are tiny, & really only suitable for one 2-3 man bushwalkng tent.

I (via phone) enquired if it was possible to to stay the night before my walk begins.

The Prom overnight hiking fee is $7.50 per person per night. (Fair, & in line with other Australuan states' NP fees.

The Prom's Overnight Hikers' Campsite fee is set at the SERIOUSLY EXPENSE (EXTORTIONATE) fee of $29.90 per night!!

AMAZINGLY CRAZY!

While I'm on a bit of an insightful rave . . . , I been coming to The Prom for over 40 years, cumulatively staying months (1st up as a young adult, & secondly with a young family of many weeks during peak past years.

I physically visited as a day visitor yesterday, with the last time I visited being 2 years ago.

Re: 34th Avenue - It is very sad to see the LACK shady vegetation.
Trees are stunted & struggling, compared to the beauty of 15 - 20 years ago.

Victoria can do better.
Nice place to access multiple walks and barbeques and seating space as well

Facilities are good, but can be cleaner. They were much cleaner last time I visited (about 10 years ago).
We saw some fishermen and visitors creating a little bit of mess which is a shame since this place is absolutely beautiful.
Overall, the staff do a good job of maintaining the place. I started by mentioning negative points, but the place is as close as it gets to heaven if all visitors take responsibility in keeping it clean.

Such a good place to stay, the facilities are clean and also never too far from the pitches, the visitors Center is also very helpful and the bbqs on the site are very handy, saw plenty of wombats too which was super exciting, 10/10 stay
Comfortable campground with basic utilities
sehr schöner Campingplatz im national Park. alles da was man als Camper braucht.
Awesome place.
Best place in the world
Great campground, definitely worth the drive. Very hard to get in though, because of the thing where you might get selected
Great facilities and amazing wildlife. You’ll definitely see wombats and wallabies. Recommend not leaving any food or rubbish in accessible locations as the wildlife will get to it!
Amazing experience. Saw this weird dog, but he seemed nice.
I think $15.50 for a cold chicken schnitzel burger is a little overpriced.
camped here countless times and have given me some of my best memories 6/5
A long way in but worth the trip.
nice plce to explore

Absolute stunning place, breathtaking views.
Unpowered camp-site was good for big groups.
Had fun camping plus the bathrooms were pretty clean ngl would stay again
Practically glamping in huge bushy sites close to the ocean and river. Wildlife everywhere ❤️ Amenities included toilets, showers sinks for dishes,  washing machines and dryers ($4 per wash or dry).
Large camp ground for all types of camping.

Price: $32 per night  For a tent  There is a lot of space  There are few electrical points.  I intend to stay for two nights.  Unfortunately there was heavy rain and strong winds.  So I decided to just sleep for one night.  But it is considered a very beautiful and natural place.  Will come back next time.
Really worth the price, I've seen lot of wild animals ! Very great place and beautiful beach behind the campground.
Perfect camp ground.
Very nice place to visit 😊 😀
A beautiful place. Truly wonderful.
Very clean Campground with great amenities (hot showers, clean drinking water, bbqs). Direct access to the beach but can be quite windy. Great for caravans and tent-ers alike

Parked at Tidal River, hiked southern circuit, 2 nights at halfway hut. If day-walking in middle of the year, take a torch. Saw plenty of people walking out after sunset. No streetlights on trails! Whole Prom beautiful place.
Awesome hiking-trip!

First time here.  HUGE area.  Booked on line, easy to find alloted site.  Easy walk to beach.  General store with food, supermarket,  souvenirs and fishing stuff.  Amenities were excellent, clean and plenty of hot water.  Very enjoyable peaceful stay.  Thankyou.
Looks great, a quick look around plenty of camping ⛺️
On peut y voir des wombats 😍😍😍
